team_context.competitive_window: retooling — went 6-11 in 2025, missed playoffs for first time since 2014, but Mahomes-era core still intact. Treating 2026 as a bounce-back year with aggressive FA spending (Walker III, Gilman, Tonga, Thornton re-sign, Kelce return).
team_context.cap_situation: tight — entered offseason $57M over cap, restructured Mahomes ($43.6M savings), released Taylor ($20M), traded McDuffie ($13.6M), now ~$21M in space per OTC. Jones restructure likely pending. Still constrained with massive future cap hits from Mahomes/Jones restructures.
team_context.trade_posture: Buyers at skill positions and secondary, but cap-constrained. Traded McDuffie for picks out of financial necessity. Would consider trading veterans with high cap hits if the return is right. Have 9th overall pick — could trade down for capital.

players.name: Patrick Mahomes
players.performance_evidence: 3x Super Bowl champion, 2x MVP. Was on pace for strong season in 2025 before torn ACL in Week 15. Fastest to 300 career TDs in NFL history. Still the consensus best QB in football when healthy.

trade_value.reasoning: Franchise QB, face of the NFL. Even recovering from ACL at 30, Mahomes is the most valuable trade asset in football. Contract has no-trade clause and massive dead cap makes trade impossible.

players.availability_reasoning: No-trade clause in contract. $200M+ in dead cap. Face of the franchise. Zero chance of trade under any circumstances.
players.injury_notes: Torn ACL and LCL in December 2025. Targeting Week 1 2026 return but timeline uncertain. Surgery in mid-December, recovery pushing toward potential Week 1 availability.

players.name: Chris Oladokun
players.performance_evidence: Started 2 games in 2025 (Weeks 16-17) after Mahomes injury. Limited NFL experience. Emergency depth piece, not a viable starter.

trade_value.reasoning: Minimal trade value. Seventh-round pick with extremely limited starting experience. Worth a conditional late-round pick at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth QB with minimal value. Chiefs would move him for any draft compensation. Likely replaced if a better backup option emerges.

players.name: Kenneth Walker III
players.performance_evidence: Super Bowl LX MVP. Led NFL in postseason rushing (313 yards, 4 TDs in 3 games). 1,050+ rushing yards in 2025 regular season while splitting carries. One of the NFL's most explosive runners — 6th-most 10+ yard runs in 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, elite RB on a new deal with 3 years of control, but RB position limits value. Super Bowl MVP pedigree and explosive play style command a mid-2nd. Heavy guaranteed money makes trade complicated.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed as the marquee free agent addition 3 days ago. Cornerstone of the 2026 offensive identity. Zero chance of trade.
players.injury_notes: Missed 10 combined games over first 3 seasons with various injuries, but played all 20 games in 2025 while splitting carries.

players.name: Brashard Smith
players.performance_evidence: 2025 5th-round pick. Limited rookie contributions as a change-of-pace back. Developmental piece behind Walker.

trade_value.reasoning: Day 3 rookie with minimal proven production. Worth a conditional late-round pick swap at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Cheap depth piece. Chiefs would move him for any meaningful pick, but there's little demand.

players.name: Rashee Rice
players.performance_evidence: 938 yards/7 TDs as rookie in 2023. 571 yards/6 TDs in just 8 games in 2025 despite missing 6 games to suspension and final games to concussion. Top-tier WR talent when available — led Chiefs WRs in every category in 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite talent on a cheap final-year rookie deal, BUT massive red flags: facing potential 10-12 game suspension for second conduct policy violation, civil lawsuit for domestic abuse, on probation. Talent says early 2nd, but availability/character risk craters value to a mid-3rd at best. Many teams won't touch him.

players.availability_reasoning: Contract year, facing potential lengthy suspension for second personal conduct violation, civil lawsuit for domestic abuse. Beat reporters say Chiefs unlikely to extend him. If a team offered a 3rd-round pick, KC would take it — but few teams will given the legal cloud.
players.injury_notes: Torn ACL in 2024 Week 4 (accidental collision with Mahomes). Concussion ended 2025 season early. Missed significant time in both seasons.
players.character_notes: Six-game suspension in 2025 for 2024 vehicle crash (pleaded guilty, 30 days jail, 5 years probation). Now facing $1M civil lawsuit from ex-girlfriend alleging repeated domestic abuse. NFL investigating for potential second conduct policy violation — could face 10-12 game suspension.
players.trade_buzz: SI reported Chiefs would consider a 2nd-round pick but acknowledge no team will offer that given legal situation. Most reporters frame his time in KC as likely ending after 2026 regardless.

players.name: Xavier Worthy
players.performance_evidence: 59 catches/638 yards/6 TDs as a rookie in 2024, plus 19 catches/287 yards/3 TDs in playoffs. 2025 was disappointing — struggled with injuries (shoulder, ankle) and beating man coverage. Arrowhead Pride described his sophomore year as poor. Still has elite speed (4.21 40-yard dash) and upside.

trade_value.reasoning: 22-year-old 1st-round WR on a cheap rookie deal with 2 years left plus 5th-year option. Disappointing 2025 limits current value, but age and contract control keep him in the late-2nd range. Teams would bet on the speed and ceiling.

players.availability_reasoning: Not being shopped, but if a team offered a high 2nd-round pick, Chiefs would have to consider given their WR room needs an overhaul anyway. Still viewed as a key part of the future, but development concerns are real.
players.injury_notes: Dislocated shoulder in Week 1 2025 collision with Travis Kelce, missed multiple games. Also dealt with ankle injury.

players.name: Tyquan Thornton
players.performance_evidence: Career-high 438 receiving yards and 3 TDs on just 19 receptions in 2025 (23.1 yards/catch). Mahomes had 121.2 passer rating when targeting Thornton. Led NFL leaders with 26.9-yard average depth of target. Deep threat specialist, also contributed 475 kick-return yards.

trade_value.reasoning: Niche deep-threat role player on a modest 2-year deal. Small sample of production limits value. Worth a 4th-round pick to a team needing a speed element.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed 3 days ago. Chiefs want him involved more in 2026. Not actively available, but could be moved for a Day 2 pick if a better WR option materializes.
players.injury_notes: Ended 2025 season on IR with concussion. No long-term concerns reported.

players.name: Travis Kelce
players.performance_evidence: 11x Pro Bowler, 7x All-Pro, 3x Super Bowl champion. 76 catches/851 yards/5 TDs in 2025 — his fewest catches since 2015 but still led Chiefs in all receiving categories. Named to Pro Bowl for 11th time. Clear decline from peak but still productive.

trade_value.reasoning: Future Hall of Famer on a 1-year deal, likely his final season. Age (36, turning 37 in October) limits trade value significantly. A contender might give a 3rd for one year of Kelce, but the Chiefs just re-signed him specifically to bounce back — they won't move him.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed on a 1-year deal specifically to return for 2026 bounce-back. Franchise icon, Mahomes' most trusted target. Turned down more money elsewhere to stay. Zero chance of trade.
players.injury_notes: No major injuries in 2025. Durability has been exceptional — has missed minimal time throughout career. Age-related decline is the primary concern.
players.trade_buzz: Considered retirement this offseason. Turned down more money from other teams to return to KC on a loyalty deal.

players.name: Noah Gray
players.performance_evidence: Just 178 receiving yards and 0 TDs in 2025 — a sharp decline. Primarily a blocking TE. Spotrac noted he'd be an easy candidate for a pay cut with incentives.

trade_value.reasoning: Minimal receiving production, overpaid for his role. Worth a 6th-round pick at most. Releasing him saves ~$4M in cap space.

players.availability_reasoning: Cut candidate for cap savings (~$4M). Beat reporters listed him as a potential release. With Kelce returning, Gray's role shrinks further. Chiefs would trade him for any draft pick.
players.trade_buzz: Listed as potential cap casualty by ESPN, Arrowhead Pride, and Spotrac in offseason previews.

players.name: Josh Simmons
players.performance_evidence: 2025 1st-round pick from Ohio State. Played like a top-10 LT as a rookie per Arrowhead Pride when healthy. Was considered a top-10 talent before patellar tendon tear at Ohio State dropped him to 32. All-Pro upside. Chiefs view him as a 'steal.'

trade_value.reasoning: 23-year-old LT with All-Pro ceiling on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years plus 5th-year option. Premium position, premium age, premium contract. Worth a late 1st despite only one NFL season.

players.availability_reasoning: Cornerstone of the OL rebuild. One of 5 players listed in the Chiefs' untouchable tier by Arrowhead Pride. Long-term LT answer on a team-friendly rookie deal.
players.injury_notes: Tore patellar tendon at Ohio State in October 2024, causing draft slide. Recovered to play as a rookie in 2025. Must be monitored.

players.name: Kingsley Suamataia
players.performance_evidence: Started all 17 games at LG in 2025 after rocky 2024 at LT (benched after 2 starts). 65.1 PFF overall grade — 36th of 81 guards (average). Showed growth throughout the season. Breakout game vs. Ravens (88.2 PFF pass-blocking grade in Week 4).

trade_value.reasoning: Young guard on a cheap rookie deal with starter upside but inconsistent production. Worth a 4th-round pick — the upside and contract control offset the average play.

players.availability_reasoning: Still developing, on a cheap deal. Chiefs need OL depth. Would require a strong offer (3rd-round pick) to consider moving him.

players.name: Creed Humphrey
players.performance_evidence: Highest-paid center in NFL ($18M AAV). 3x Pro Bowler, 1x 2nd-team All-Pro. Best center in the game since Jason Kelce retired per PFF. Hasn't missed a start. Allowed pressure on just 2.4% of dropbacks in 2023.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite center in his prime on a market-setting deal. 27 years old with 3 years of control. IOL position limits value vs. OT, but Humphrey is the best in the business. Worth a late 1st.

players.availability_reasoning: Cornerstone of the offensive line. Elite production, prime age, long-term deal. Arrowhead Pride lists him among the 5 untouchable Chiefs players.

players.name: Trey Smith
players.performance_evidence: Highest-paid guard in NFL history ($94M/4yr, $70M guaranteed). 2024 Pro Bowler. One of just 5 OL with fewer than 10 total sacks allowed over 2,500+ pass-blocking snaps over 5 seasons. Started 80 of 81 career games. Ranked No. 4 overall interior lineman by ESPN entering 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite guard in his prime on a massive but market-appropriate deal. 3 years of control. The contract size limits trade appeal — acquiring team takes on $24M+ cap hits — but the talent is undeniable. Worth a late 1st to a team that can absorb the contract.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed record-setting extension in July 2025. Cornerstone of OL alongside Humphrey. $38M dead cap makes trade essentially impossible.
players.injury_notes: Blood clot history (2018) monitored but has had zero issues since entering NFL. Has been remarkably durable.

players.name: Jaylon Moore
players.performance_evidence: Signed 2yr/$30M deal in 2025 FA after filling in as 49ers' LT (replacing Trent Williams). Started all 17 games in 2025. Serviceable but unspectacular — part of an OL that ranked poorly in run blocking. Moved to RT after Taylor release and Simmons emergence at LT.

trade_value.reasoning: Below-average starter on a pricey 1-year remaining deal ($15M guaranteed). Limited trade appeal given the contract. Worth a late 4th at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Arrowhead Pride listed him as a potential savings candidate. His $15M salary is fully guaranteed so release doesn't help, but a trade could clear cap space. Would move for a 4th-round pick.

players.name: George Karlaftis
players.performance_evidence: 30.5 career sacks through 4 seasons. 6.0 sacks in 2025, 10.5 in 2023, 8.0 in 2024. 66 total pressures (27th in NFL per PFF) in 2025. Dominant in 2024 playoffs (4 sacks, 8 QB hits in 3 games). Signed 4yr/$93M extension ($62M guaranteed) in July 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old EDGE on a long-term deal with 5 years remaining at a below-market cap hit ($15.2M in 2026). Elite age/contract/position combination. Worth a mid-1st round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed massive extension. Only 25, ascending player. $26.9M dead cap. Core defensive building block alongside Jones. Zero chance of trade.

players.name: Chris Jones
players.performance_evidence: Highest-paid non-QB defender in NFL ($44.85M cap hit). Consistently elite interior pass rusher — 10.5 sacks in 2023, maintained high pressure rate through 2025. Still playing at All-Pro level despite turning 32. Played an insane amount of snaps for a DT his age in 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: Still elite but massive contract and $64.25M dead cap make trade functionally impossible. If somehow traded, the acquiring team gets elite DL play but at the highest price tag for any non-QB. The dead cap alone is a dealbreaker.

players.availability_reasoning: $64.25M dead cap if traded — literally untradeable. $35M in guaranteed 2026 salary ensures he stays. Some observers have floated a trade idea, but the dead cap and guaranteed money make it impossible without a complete tear-down.
players.injury_notes: Heavy workload concern — played excessive snap count for a 31-year-old DT in 2025. Chiefs need more DL depth to preserve him.
players.trade_buzz: Some analysts have suggested Chiefs should consider trading Jones while he's still elite, but $64M dead cap and $35M guaranteed salary make this impossible.

players.name: Khyiris Tonga
players.performance_evidence: Had a career year in New England in 2025. Run-stuffing nose tackle who provides strength and depth behind Chris Jones. Signed 3yr/$21M deal ($14M guaranteed) in 2026 FA.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid run-stuffing DT on a fair deal. Just signed, so not moving, but intrinsic value is around a 4th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed in free agency to address a critical need behind Jones. Not going anywhere in 2026 unless an unexpected offer materializes.

players.name: Felix Anudike-Uzomah
players.performance_evidence: Missed entire 2025 season with hamstring injury. Only 2 career starts since being drafted 31st overall in 2023. Major disappointment — Chiefs expected to decline his 5th-year option for 2027.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 1st-round pick with very little production and a lost season. Some team might take a flyer on the pedigree for a 5th or 6th-round pick, but the lack of production is concerning.

players.availability_reasoning: Chiefs expected to decline 5th-year option. Entering final year of rookie deal. If a team offers a Day 3 pick for a change-of-scenery candidate, Chiefs would accept.
players.injury_notes: Missed entire 2025 season with hamstring injury. Has only started 2 games in 3 NFL seasons.
players.trade_buzz: Arrowhead Pride and Spotrac both note Chiefs will decline 5th-year option. Effectively a one-year rental at this point.

players.name: Drue Tranquill
players.performance_evidence: In on 85% of defensive snaps in 2025. Solid, experienced linebacker who pairs with Bolton. Not flashy but reliable — classic Spagnuolo system LB.

trade_value.reasoning: Aging LB on an expiring deal with no guarantees. Worth a 5th-round pick at most. More likely a cap casualty than trade piece.

players.availability_reasoning: Listed as a cap casualty candidate by ESPN, NFL.com, and multiple beat reporters. $6M in non-guaranteed savings. Chiefs drafted Jeffrey Bassa (LB) in 2025 as potential replacement. Release is more likely than trade.
players.trade_buzz: Multiple outlets listed Tranquill as a prime cut candidate for $6M in cap savings.

players.name: Nick Bolton
players.performance_evidence: Led Chiefs with 106 tackles and 11 TFLs in 2024. Career highs in sacks (3) and PDs (6). Set franchise record with 180 tackles in 2022. Led KC in tackles 3 of 4 seasons. Three-down LB with pass-rush ability — exactly what Spagnuolo wants.

trade_value.reasoning: 26-year-old above-average LB on a 2-year deal at $15M AAV. LB position limits trade value, but Bolton is a complete player. $15M guaranteed in 2026 means the acquiring team gets a known commodity. Worth a late 2nd.

players.availability_reasoning: Core defensive player who just signed his extension in March 2025. Not being shopped, but high cap hit ($19.25M) means he has a price. Would require a 2nd-round pick to pry away.
players.injury_notes: Missed half of 2023 with wrist injury (8 games, 60 tackles). Has been fully healthy since.

players.name: Cooper McDonald
players.performance_evidence: UDFA who has carved out a special teams and rotational role. Limited defensive snap count. Solid depth piece.

trade_value.reasoning: Minimal trade value. Rotational LB on a minimum deal. Not worth pursuing in trade.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth piece with no dead cap implications. Would move for any draft pick.

players.name: Nohl Williams
players.performance_evidence: Described as 'one of the best young cornerbacks in football' by A to Z Sports after his rookie year. Filled in at perimeter CB during 2025 and played well enough to be named CB1 going into 2026 after McDuffie/Watson departures. Day 2 rookie who outperformed expectations.

trade_value.reasoning: 23-year-old CB on a cheap Day 2 rookie deal with 3 years of control and promising production. Premium position. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd given age, position, and contract.

players.availability_reasoning: Projected as starting CB1 after trading McDuffie and losing Watson in FA. Chiefs desperately need him. Would require an overpay (late 1st) to consider moving him.

players.name: Kristian Fulton
players.performance_evidence: Signed 2yr/$20M deal in 2025 FA after starting 14 games for Chargers in 2024. Proved capable when healthy in 2025. Strong finish to the season secured his roster spot per Arrowhead Pride. Reduced to reserve role at times.

trade_value.reasoning: Average starter on an expiring deal with a $13M cap hit and $5M guaranteed. The guaranteed money means you're paying him regardless. Worth a late 4th to a CB-needy contender.

players.availability_reasoning: Chiefs would save ~$5M by releasing him (post-June 1). With McDuffie/Watson gone, he's needed, but if a trade partner absorbs the salary, KC would consider. Not being actively shopped, but has a reasonable price.
players.injury_notes: Injury history is a concern — has missed time in multiple seasons. Arrowhead Pride noted 'given Fulton's injury history, adding depth would make sense.'
players.trade_buzz: Listed as a potential cap savings candidate by multiple outlets, but departures of McDuffie and Watson make releasing him unlikely.

players.name: Chris Roland-Wallace
players.performance_evidence: Young nickel corner/safety hybrid. Coming off injury. In the mix for nickel position along with Kevin Knowles II. Unproven but has intriguing versatility.

trade_value.reasoning: Unproven UDFA on a minimum deal. Minimal trade value — essentially a camp body with upside.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth piece competing for a roster spot. Would move for any draft pick.
players.injury_notes: Coming off injury per reporting. Availability for 2026 training camp to be monitored.

players.name: Chamarri Conner
players.performance_evidence: Versatile safety who played significant snaps in 2025. Part of the young secondary group the Chiefs went with after older veterans departed. Mixed results — strong at times but inconsistent. Listed in the 'show me more' tier by Arrowhead Pride.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, cheap safety on a final-year rookie deal. Modest production but good contract value. Worth a late 4th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Final year of rookie deal. Chiefs signed Gilman to be the new FS starter, which could push Conner into a box safety role. Available for fair value but not being actively shopped.

players.name: Alohi Gilman
players.performance_evidence: Started all 17 games for first time in 2025 (split between Chargers and Ravens). 90 tackles, 9 PDs, 1 INT, 2 fumble recoveries including 84-yard TD return. PFF ranked 17th in zone coverage snaps among safeties. Stabilized Ravens secondary after midseason trade.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starting safety on a fair 3-year deal. Just signed as a targeted free agent acquisition. Worth a mid-3rd, but not going anywhere.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed to replace Bryan Cook. Key piece of the secondary rebuild. Would require a significant overpay to move.
players.injury_notes: Laundry list of minor injuries throughout career — missed games in 2021, 2023, and 2024 with various soft tissue issues. 2025 was his first fully healthy season.

players.name: Harrison Butker
players.performance_evidence: One of the most accurate kickers in NFL history. 33/38 on FGs in 2025. 3x Super Bowl champion. Made clutch kicks throughout Chiefs dynasty run. Signed extension in 2024.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite kicker but kickers carry minimal trade value by convention. Worth a 5th-round pick theoretically.

players.availability_reasoning: Elite, franchise kicker on a team-friendly deal. No reason to move him.
players.character_notes: Controversial public political/religious statements have drawn media attention but have not affected his standing with the organization.
